measurement_note: Size is N, the friend count. The first half ranks every second-half friend before its partner, while second-half friends rank partners first; no friend is unhappy, but half the friends inspect half the population. The Accepted rank table and independent inverse-rank maps use O(N^2) time. A correct slower implementation performs explicit Python-level linear preference scans for both ranks on every candidate, uses O(N^3) time, completes all 16/32/64 tiers, and is rejected only by scaling. A draft using built-in list.index was discarded because optimized C-level scans did not produce a calibrated slower trend.
